No Poverty.reason: The course covers financial services and property (Target 1.4) but lacks an explicit focus on poverty eradication or ensuring access for the poor and vulnerable.

Zero Hunger.reason: No evidence in the course description or schedule relates to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No evidence in the course description or schedule relates to health, well-being, or disease prevention.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3). The explicit mention of 'sustainability considerations' aligns with education for sustainable development (Target 4.7).
evidence: understand and be able to identify the ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability considerations of business situations.

Gender Equality.reason: The course covers property ownership and financial services, which are mechanisms relevant to Target 5.a, but it lacks an explicit focus on gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence in the course description or schedule relates to clean water, sanitation, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No evidence in the course description or schedule relates to energy, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly covers 'real estate financial system' and 'real estate financial products,' directly supporting Target 8.10 on strengthening financial institutions and access to financial services.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course focuses on 'real estate development' and 'investment,' which is fundamental to developing quality, reliable, and sustainable infrastructure (Target 9.1).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course covers 'real estate financial system' and 'risk analysis,' which tangentially relates to Target 10.5 on regulating financial markets, but lacks a direct focus on reducing inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course on 'Real Estate Investment' and 'real estate development,' combined with 'sustainability considerations,' directly addresses housing and sustainable urbanization (Targets 11.1, 11.3).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The core competency to 'understand and be able to identify the ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability considerations of business situations' directly aligns with encouraging sustainable corporate practices (Target 12.6) in the real estate sector.
evidence: understand and be able to identify the ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability considerations of business situations.

Climate Action.reason: The course's focus on 'risk analysis' and 'sustainability considerations' can be inferred to include climate-related risks to real estate, relevant to strengthening resilience (Target 13.1).

Life Below Water.reason: No evidence in the course description or schedule relates to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable use of marine resources.

Life on Land.reason: The study of 'real estate development' and 'sustainability considerations' implicitly relates to land use planning and integrating ecosystem values into development processes (Target 15.9).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course explicitly covers 'Real estate law and taxation' (不動產法制與稅賦), directly supporting the promotion of the rule of law (Target 16.3) and transparent institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's analysis of financial systems and risk contributes to understanding macroeconomic stability (Target 17.13), and its inclusion of 'sustainability considerations' supports policy coherence for sustainable development (Target 17.14).
